full_prompt: Design a monolithic cryogenic LOX (liquid oxygen) propellant tank for a student-developed LOX/LCH4 bipropellant rocket with total-impulse budget under 200,000 lb-sec. The tank is a seamless 316L stainless steel pressure vessel, spin-formed hemispherical domes, internal diameter 200 mm, cylindrical barrel length 600 mm, one forward fill/vent AN-8 port on the forward dome and one aft feed AN-12 port on the aft dome, both welded bosses integral to domes (welds permitted for cryogenic tanks but must be X-ray inspected). Propellant: LOX at 90 K (boiling). Maximum Operating Pressure (MOP) = 30 bar (3 MPa). Material 316L stainless steel at 90 K (cryogenic derating): Young's modulus 210 GPa, yield strength 450 MPa at 90 K (austenitic steel strengthens at cryogenic temperature), ultimate tensile strength 720 MPa at 90 K, density 8000 kg/m^3. Requirements: (R1) proof pressure 1.5 x MOP = 45 bar held for 5 minutes; max von Mises at proof <= yield / 1.1 = 409 MPa; (R2) burst pressure 2.5 x MOP = 75 bar; max von Mises at burst <= ultimate / 1.25 = 576 MPa; (R3) all welds shall be full-penetration and inspected to ASME BPVC Sec IX; fatigue life for the mission (10 pressurization cycles) shall satisfy stress range margin >= 2.0 (stress range <= fatigue endurance / 2.0); (R4) dry mass <= 6.5 kg; (R5) during LOX loading the external tank skin shall not exceed 100 C nor drop below -200 C on instrumented points; (R6) thermal contraction from 293 K to 90 K shall not produce max von Mises exceeding 250 MPa at mounting lugs. Primary verification: structural FEA at proof (45 bar at 90 K), burst (75 bar at 90 K), plus a coupled thermal-structural step cooling from 293 K to 90 K; vibration analysis NOT required at this gate (first mission gate is pressure qualification); fluid and radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242, weld map, and a report listing wall thickness, dry mass, max von Mises at proof and burst, thermal-contraction lug stress, and mass in kg.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic cryogenic LOX tank for a student LOX/LCH4 bipropellant rocket (total-impulse budget under 200,000 lb-sec). The tank is a seamless 316L stainless steel pressure vessel with spin-formed hemispherical domes, one forward AN-8 fill/vent boss and one aft AN-12 feed boss, both welded integral to the domes (welds permitted but to be X-rayed per ASME BPVC Sec IX). Propellant is LOX at 90 K; MOP = 30 bar. Fatigue life target is 10 pressurization cycles with a stress-range margin >= 2.0.

Envelope: internal diameter 200 mm, cylindrical barrel length 600 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 proof: 1.5 x MOP = 45 bar at 90 K, held for 5 minutes.
- LC2 burst: 2.5 x MOP = 75 bar at 90 K.
- LC3 thermal contraction: uniform cooldown from 293 K to 90 K; check lug / mount region stress.
- LC4 LOX loading: instrumented skin temperature must stay within -200 C to +100 C.

prompt.title: LOX cryogenic propellant tank (student bipropellant rocket)
prompt.brief: 316L stainless LOX tank, MOP 30 bar, 90 K service. Proof 1.5x, burst 2.5x MOP. Includes coupled cryogenic thermal-structural contraction case.

geometric_constraints.welds: full-penetration, X-ray inspected (allowed for cryogenic tanks)

load_cases.name: thermal_skin_limits
load_cases.description: loading transient; external skin within [-200, 100] C

boundary_conditions.mounting: rear feed boss + mid-body stringer lugs fixed
boundary_conditions.thermal: inner wall held at 90 K; outer wall free convection with insulation layer

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Sloshing out of scope; tank is hydrostatic for qualification.
exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Pressure qualification gate precedes dynamic qualification.

eval_coverage: cryogenic property derating (austenitic steel at 90 K)
